The render call chain and the per-call unit 25 26 File `src/Web/Theory.hs`: 27 28 * `htmlThyPath` dispatches the pane routes: 29 * `TheoryMessage -> pp $ messageSnippet thy` (the `main/message` pane) 30 * `TheoryRules -> pp $ rulesSnippet thy` (the `main/rules` pane) 31 * `messageSnippet thy = vcat [ ppSection "Signature" [prettySignatureWithMaude ŌĆ”] 32 , ppSection "Construction Rules" (map prettyRuleAC ŌĆ”) 33 , ppSection "Deconstruction Rules" (map prettyRuleAC ŌĆ”) ]` ŌĆö the whole pane is 34 **one** `HtmlDoc Doc`, a `vcat` of all sections. `ppSection` wraps each body in 35 `withTag "p" [("class","monospace rules")] (vcat (intersperse (text "") s))`. 36 * `rulesSnippet` is analogous: one `vcat` of `Macros`, `Fact Symbols ŌĆ”`, 37 `Multiset Rewriting Rules` (`map prettyClosedProtoRule` + intruder AC rules), 38 `Restrictions ŌĆ”`. 39 * `pp d = renderHtmlDoc d` (local `where`), and `renderHtmlDoc` lives in 40 `lib/utils/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs`: 41 `renderHtmlDoc = postprocessHtmlDoc . render . getHtmlDoc`. 42 * `render` (`lib/utils/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Class.hs`) = `P.render` = 43 HughesPJ `render = renderStyle style`, with 44 `style = Style{ lineLength = 100, ribbonsPerLine = 1.5, mode = PageMode }` and 45 `ribbonLen = round (100 / 1.5) = 67`. Pair / AC / application delimiter drops ŌĆö same mechanism 178 179 The "delimiter collapsing to a space" (`ŌĆ”>) )` vs `ŌĆ”>)`) is the identical 180 escaped-width effect applied to the pair/AC/application `fcat` fills (the `<`, 181 `>` delimiters and `'ŌĆ”'` string constants are all fill/text tokens measured at 182 escaped width). Same. 192 193 So the `>`/`)` drops the sealed sweep flagged are not a separate bug; they are the 194 pair/AC/app fills breaking one item earlier under escaped-width measurement. 195 196 ### A7. The signature-vs-rule "width tension" ŌĆö dissolved 197 198 There is **no** per-section width difference. All sections share one pass at 199 (100, 67). The apparent tension was: 200 * signature `functions:` / `builtins:` fills reach absolute column 78 201 (= nest 11 + 67) ŌåÆ looked like it needed width Ōēź 78, ribbon 67; 202 * rule bodies wrap at display content 66 (nest 3) ŌåÆ looked like it needed 203 effective width Ōēł 67. 204 205 Resolution: signature `functions:`/`builtins:` fill **items carry no entities** 206 (`name/arity`, builtin names), so their escaped width equals their display width; 207 they wrap exactly at the true ribbon 67 from their nest, and (100, 67) reproduces 208 all 139 of them (as the sealed room found). Rule bodies and equation pairs *do* 209 carry entities (`]->`, `-->`, `<ŌĆ”>` pairs, `'ŌĆ”'` constants), so they are measured 210 wider than they display and wrap "early" in display terms. One uniform style; the 211 only variable is how many escapable characters a given line contains. 212 213 Per-family render table (all identical ŌĆö that is the finding): 214 215 | pane / family | render unit | width | ribbon | measured width basis | 216 |---|---|---|---|---| 217 | message: Signature | whole pane (one pass) | 100 | 67 | HTML-escaped | 218 | message: Construction/Deconstruction rules | whole pane (one pass) | 100 | 67 | HTML-escaped | 219 | rules: Macros / Fact symbols / MSR rules / Restrictions | whole pane (one pass) | 100 | 67 | HTML-escaped | 220 221 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 222 ## PART B ŌĆö sealed-safe behavioral instruction block 223 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 224 225 (For the sealed implementer who has read BEHAVIOR.md "Web mode (R6)". This block 226 contains only observable rendering laws and numbers. Read it in place of Part A.) 227 228 **B0. Setting.** All the web-mode wrap laws below hold at the parameters you 229 already pinned: page width **100**, ribbon **67**, one layout pass over the whole 230 pane document (the signature block and every rule/restriction are laid out in the 231 same single pass; there is no per-rule or per-section re-parameterisation, and the 232 ribbon does not narrow as the document gets deeper ŌĆö each line is judged 233 independently from its own indentation column). Keep width 100 / ribbon 67; do 234 **not** introduce per-family widths. 235 236 **B1. The one law you are missing: measure the ESCAPED width, not the display 237 width.** In web mode, the width the layout engine charges for each character is 238 its width *after* the entity escaping you already apply ŌĆö i.e. the layout must be 239 computed on the escaped form, not on the glyphs. Concretely, when deciding whether 240 something fits on a line, count each character as: 241 242 | character | columns charged to layout | 243 |---|---| 244 | `<` | 4 | 245 | `>` | 4 | 246 | `&` | 5 | 247 | `"` | 6 | 248 | `'` | 5 | 249 | any other visible character | 1 | 250 251 Leading indentation is still free of the ribbon (measured as the indent, as 252 today). Only web layout shifts. 260 261 **B2.
